description: The Islamic Consultative Assembly (Majles-e Shura-ye Eslami), commonly known as the Majlis or Majles, is the unicameral legislature of the Islamic Republic of Iran. It currently has 290 seats, elected for four-year terms. All candidates must be vetted and approved by the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council). The Majlis can legislate, ratify treaties, and impeach the president, but all legislation must pass Guardian Council review for conformity with Islamic law and the constitution.
editorial_notes: 12 sessions since 1980. Current (12th) session began 2024. Located at Baharestan Square, Tehran.
